name: AtriCure, Inc.
business: AtriCure is a leading innovator in surgical treatments and therapies for atrial fibrillation, left atrial appendage management and post-operative pain management. The company sells its products to medical centers through a direct sales force in the United States, Germany, France, the United Kingdom, the Benelux region, Australia and Canada, and through distributors in other international markets.

exposures.sentence: We are a leading innovator in surgical treatments and therapies for atrial fibrillation (Afib or AF), left atrial appendage (LAA) management and post-operative pain management.

catalysts.event: FDA 510(k) clearance for AtriClip PRO-Mini LAA Exclusion System
catalysts.sentence: During the first quarter of 2025, FDA granted 510(k) clearance for the AtriClip PRO-Mini LAA Exclusion System.

catalysts.event: FDA 510(k) clearance for cryoICE cryoXT probe
catalysts.sentence: During the second quarter of 2025, FDA granted 510(k) clearance for the cryoICE cryoXT probe, a cryoablation device designed specifically for Cryo Nerve Block therapy to alleviate pain in amputation patients.

catalysts.event: Launch of cryoXT probe in the United States
catalysts.sentence: During the third quarter of 2025, this device was launched in the United States.

catalysts.event: Completed enrollment of LeAAPS clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, we completed trial enrollment of 6,573 patients across 139 centers globally.

catalysts.event: First patient enrollment in BoxX-NoAF clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: during October 2025, we completed the first patient enrollment.

catalysts.event: First-in-human treatments with dual energy platform (PFA and Advanced RFA)
catalysts.sentence: During the fourth quarter of 2025, we executed successful first-in-human treatments using our novel dual energy platform that integrates Pulsed Field Ablation (PFA) with Advanced Radiofrequency Ablation (Advanced RFA).

tamClaims.claim: Afib affects over 59 million people worldwide
tamClaims.sentence: Afib is an irregular heartbeat, or arrhythmia, which affects over 59 million people worldwide

tamClaims.claim: Over four million people in the United States currently suffer from long-standing persistent Afib
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that over four million people in the United States currently suffer from long-standing persistent Afib.

tamClaims.claim: Over one million diagnoses annually in the United States alone
tamClaims.sentence: Afib is the most commonly diagnosed sustained cardiac arrhythmia, with over one million diagnoses annually in the United States alone.

tamClaims.claim: Over 500,000 patients undergoing open-heart surgery annually are potential candidates for surgical ablation
tamClaims.sentence: Of the patients undergoing open-heart surgery globally on an annual basis, we estimate that over 500,000 are potential candidates for surgical ablation using our products, as they have pre-operative Afib.

tamClaims.claim: Less than 15% of those candidates are being treated with surgical ablation
tamClaims.sentence: Today, we estimate that less than 15% of those candidates are being treated with surgical ablation.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 500,000 Afib patients are treated by catheter ablation every year in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that approximately 500,000 Afib patients are treated by catheter ablation every year in the United States, a number that is expected to see significant continued growth.

tamClaims.claim: Up to 50% of cardiac surgery patients without pre-operative Afib develop post-operative Afib
tamClaims.sentence: it is estimated that up to 50% of patients who undergo cardiac surgery who do not have diagnosed pre-operative Afib eventually develop POAF.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 150,000 thoracic procedures and 250,000 cardiothoracic procedures performed annually in the US
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that each year approximately 150,0000 thoracic procedures and approximately 250,000 cardiothoracic procedures are performed in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Over 180,000 amputations occur in the U.S. each year
tamClaims.sentence: Each year, over 180,000 amputations occur in the U.S., with approximately 60% of patients experiencing residual limb pain and up to 85% reporting phantom limb pain.
